steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence lists examples of Stigler's law from different fields (philosophy, mathematics, fluid mechanics). The blank must connect 'Exemplars of Stigler's law ______ there is Cartesian duality' with the right punctuation to introduce this list of elaborations.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'abound in philosophy;': A semicolon links two independent clauses. 'In philosophy, there is Cartesian duality; in mathematics...' would need a complete clause before the semicolon, but the main clause ends at 'abound.' Placing 'in philosophy' before the semicolon strands it in the wrong clause. ✗
  • Choice B — 'abound: in philosophy,': The colon follows the complete independent clause 'Exemplars of Stigler's law abound' and correctly introduces the list of examples that follows. The comma after 'in philosophy' then separates the first item from the rest. ✓
  • Choice C — 'abound, in philosophy,': Two commas around 'in philosophy' would create a parenthetical, implying this phrase is a non-essential aside rather than the start of the elaborating list. ✗
  • Choice D — 'abound in philosophy:': The colon comes after 'in philosophy,' making 'Exemplars of Stigler's law abound in philosophy' the preceding clause — but then the list continues with 'in mathematics' and 'in fluid mechanics,' which grammatically belong to the same series as 'in philosophy.' Separating philosophy from the rest breaks the parallel list. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
